name: PayActiv Earned Wage Access API
what_it_does: PayActiv REST API for earned wage access (EWA) and employee financial wellness platform. Enables AI agents to manage earned wage access transaction initiation and balance retrieval for on-demand pay delivery, handle employee eligibility verification and accrued wage calculation integration with payroll systems, access financial wellness tool data including savings, bill payment, and budgeting for employee benefit analytics, retrieve Visa prepaid card and bank account delivery data for payment distribution, handle employer payroll integration data for seamless reconciliation, access employee engagement and adoption analytics for benefits program management, manage wage advance repayment and payroll deduction workflow, retrieve financial stress score and wellness metric data for benefits ROI measurement, handle direct deposit split functionality for financial management, and integrate earned wage access data with HRIS, payroll, and employee benefits platforms.
use_cases: Enabling on-demand pay from employee financial wellness agents
Verifying earned wages from payroll integration agents
Tracking financial wellness from HR benefits analytics agents
Managing EWA reconciliation from payroll processing agents
not_for: Consumer lending without employer-sponsored EWA program context
High-cost consumer cash advances without employer integration
Payroll systems without earned wage access benefit program
best_when: An employer, HRIS vendor, or payroll provider using PayActiv wants AI agents to automate earned wage access eligibility, on-demand pay delivery, payroll reconciliation, and employee financial wellness program analytics.
avoid_when: REGULATORY RISK: Earned wage access regulation is evolving — CFPB proposed EWA guidance (2023) and several states (California, Nevada) have EWA-specific laws; automated EWA programs must comply with current and evolving regulatory requirements. Automated EWA eligibility based on accrued wages requires accurate payroll data integration; errors in accrued wage calculation create overpayment and payroll reconciliation issues. PayActiv programs must comply with CFPB's stance that EWA may be consumer credit in some structures.

auth.notes: PayActiv uses OAuth 2.0 and API key for employer and partner integrations. Milpitas, California HQ. VC-backed ($200M+ raised, SoftBank Vision Fund, others). CFPB pilot program participant. Walmart partnership (one of largest employers). SAP-certified integration. Webhooks for EWA transaction events. Strong in retail, healthcare, and food service sectors. Competes with DailyPay and Branch for earned wage access market.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — employer partnership required
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-transaction EWA fee; employer subscription for financial wellness platform
pricing.notes: Milpitas, California. Founded 2012. VC-backed: SoftBank Vision Fund, Eldridge Industries ($200M+ raised). CFPB pilot program participant (first EWA company). Walmart strategic partnership. 200+ million EWA transactions. 1,500+ employer clients. Strong in retail, healthcare, and food service. Competes with DailyPay (largest EWA company) and Branch for earned wage access.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; transaction volume dependent

known_agent_gotchas: REGULATORY RISK: EWA regulation is evolving — CFPB proposed rules (2023) may reclassify some EWA structures as consumer credit; automated EWA programs must monitor regulatory changes
State EWA laws — California, Nevada, Missouri, and other states have EWA-specific regulations; automated EWA must apply state-specific disclosure, fee, and repayment requirements
Payroll accuracy dependency — EWA eligibility is based on accrued wages from payroll; payroll data integration errors (missed punches, PTO inaccuracies) cause overpayment and reconciliation failures
Employer payroll timing — EWA reconciliation timing differs from payroll cycle; automated reconciliation must handle EWA advance repayment in next payroll without double-deduction
Employee consent requirements — EWA participation requires employee consent and electronic disclosure acknowledgment; automated enrollment without documented consent creates regulatory exposure
Tip and overtime calculation — accrued wage calculation for hourly workers must include tips and overtime; automated eligibility calculation must apply correct pay rules for each employee type
agent_readiness.security_notes: Earned wage access. SOC2. OAuth2/API key. US. Employee wage access and payroll data.

security_score_components.security_notes: EWA platform. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Employee wage and payroll data.
